content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
➔:!8 J Street • Sui1e 6:!0 • Sacramento, CA 9581-4-23::!9 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 3:!2-0886 
Amy J. Lepine 
Lepine Law Group 
444 West "C" Street, Suite 140 
San Diego, CA 92101 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-12-061 
Dear Ms. Lepine: 
May 29, 2012 
This letter confirms the receipt of your May 2, 2012, request for advice regarding 
a candidate for the BAME Renaissance board of directors, and a potential conflict of interest 
under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). The Act's conflict-of-interest 
provisions apply only to "public officials." (Sections 87100 & 87103; Regulation 18700(b)(l ).) 
Section 82048 defines a public official as .. every member, officer, employee or consultant of a 
state or local government agency." 
According to your letter, "BAME Renaissance, CDC, is a faith-based community 
redevelopment corporation based out of Bethel AME church in San Diego." As confirmed by 
your office on May 21, 2012, BAME is not a government entity. Therefore, neither the board 
candidate nor any current board members are public officials subject to the provisions of the Act. 
Since your inquiry presents issues which are outside the purview of the Act, your request 
for advice must be declined pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D). 
JW:jgl 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
vroYC---
oodside 
Cou sel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Since your inquiry presents issues which are outside the purview of the Act, your request for advice must be declined pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate for the board of directors of BAME Renaissance, a faith-based community redevelopment corporation, is subject to conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC declines to provide advice because BAME Renaissance is not a government entity, and therefore the board candidate and members are not public officials subject to the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear reasoning; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but substantive analysis provided; document ID formatted as W-12-061 (withdrawal/declining format)

QUESTION: Whether a candidate for the board of directors of BAME Renaissance, a faith-based community redevelopment corporation, is subject to conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Since your inquiry presents issues which are outside the purview of the Act, your request for advice must be declined pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines an advice request regarding a potential conflict of interest for a candidate on the BAME Renaissance board, finding that the organization is a faith-based community redevelopment corporation (not a government entity) and thus outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.
